skill.name: find-bugs
skill.description: Find bugs, security vulnerabilities, and code quality issues in local branch changes. Use when asked to review changes, find bugs, security review, or audit code on the current branch.
skill.summary: Find bugs, security vulnerabilities, and code quality issues in local branch changes. Use when asked...
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All static findings evaluated as false positives. External commands (git/gh CLI) are legitimate code review tooling. Network URL is documentation only. Cryptographic warnings are keyword false positives. Skill is safe for publication.

high_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm (Line 3)
high_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 'weak cryptographic algorithm' but line 3 contains only 'security vulnerabilities' in description text - no cryptographic code present
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is YAML frontmatter containing description text. No cryptographic algorithms or code present.

high_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm (Line 77)
high_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 'weak cryptographic algorithm' but line 77 contains only '* **File:Line** - Brief description' - no cryptographic code present
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 77 is output format template text. No cryptographic algorithms or code present.

medium_findings.title: False Positive: External Commands (Line 23)
medium_findings.description: git diff command with $(gh repo view...) substitution is legitimate code review tooling. gh CLI command is hardcoded, not user-controlled input.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Command substitution runs gh repo view to get default branch name - standard GitHub CLI usage for git operations, not user input injection.

medium_findings.title: False Positive: Network Hardcoded URL (Line 4)
medium_findings.description: URL in YAML frontmatter is source reference only, not an active network request
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Source URL is documentation/metadata only. No network requests are made to this URL during skill execution.

content.value_statement: This skill reviews code changes for bugs, security vulnerabilities, and quality issues using a systematic multi-phase approach. It maps attack surfaces, runs security checklists, and provides prioritized findings with evidence and fix suggestions.
actual_capabilities: Get complete git diff of branch changes against default branch
Identify attack surfaces including inputs, queries, auth checks, and external calls
Run comprehensive security checklist covering OWASP top 10 issues
Verify findings against existing tests and code context
Prioritize issues by severity with evidence and fix suggestions
Report security vulnerabilities, bugs, and code quality issues
limitations: Does not execute or test code - only static analysis of diffs
Cannot detect issues in dependencies or external services
Limited to changes on current branch vs default branch
May miss context-only issues without full repository understanding

use_cases.title: Pre-commit Code Review
use_cases.description: Run before submitting pull requests to catch bugs and security issues early
use_cases.target_user: Developers wanting to improve code quality before review

use_cases.title: Pull Request Security Audit
use_cases.description: Systematically review incoming changes for security vulnerabilities and OWASP issues

use_cases.title: Automated Quality Gate
use_cases.description: Integrate into CI/CD pipelines to block commits with critical security issues

prompt_templates.title: Basic Bug Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Find bugs in the current branch changes
prompt_templates.scenario: Simple request to review code for bugs

prompt_templates.title: Security Focused Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the changes on this branch for security vulnerabilities. Check for injection risks, authentication issues, and data exposure.

prompt_templates.title: Comprehensive Code Audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Perform a full security and quality audit of the changes on this branch. Map attack surfaces, run the security checklist, and provide prioritized findings with evidence and fixes.
prompt_templates.scenario: Complete security audit with full methodology

prompt_templates.title: Quick Quality Check
prompt_templates.prompt: Quickly scan the branch changes for critical bugs and high-severity security issues. Skip stylistic issues.
prompt_templates.scenario: Fast scan for critical issues only

output_examples.input: Find bugs in the current branch changes
output_examples.output:
## Code Review Findings

### Security Vulnerabilities

**src/auth.js:42 - SQL Injection Risk**
- Severity: Critical
- Problem: User input directly concatenated in SQL query
- Evidence: No parameterization on line 42, user.id used directly
- Fix: Use parameterized queries or ORM
- References: OWASP A03:2021

### Bugs

**src/parser.js:15 - Null Reference**
- Severity: Medium
- Problem: Attempting to access property of undefined
- Evidence: No null check before accessing user.preferences
- Fix: Add null check or use optional chaining

## Security Audit

Reviewed 12 files. Found 2 security issues:

1. **api/handler.js:28** - Command Injection (Critical)
2. **utils/crypto.js:8** - Weak Random (High)

No issues found in authentication, authorization, or data validation.

best_practices: Run before every pull request to catch issues early
Address critical and high severity findings before merging
Use findings to educate team about security patterns
Integrate into CI to prevent security regressions
anti_patterns: Ignoring findings without investigation because they seem false
Only running when explicitly asked instead of proactively
Treating all findings as equally urgent instead of prioritizing
Making changes directly instead of letting user decide

faq.question: What does this skill actually check?
faq.answer: It checks for bugs, security vulnerabilities like injection and XSS, authentication issues, authorization flaws, and general code quality problems using a systematic security checklist.

faq.question: Does it modify my code?
faq.answer: No. This skill only analyzes and reports findings. It never makes changes - you decide what to fix.

faq.question: What branch does it compare against?
faq.answer: It compares the current branch HEAD against the repository default branch (usually main or master).

faq.question: Can it find all security issues?
faq.answer: No. It performs static analysis of diffs and cannot detect runtime issues, dependency vulnerabilities, or complex logic flaws that require execution.

faq.question: Does it work with any language?
faq.answer: Yes. The security checklist is language-agnostic and applies general security principles to any code being reviewed.

faq.question: How long does a review take?
faq.answer: Time varies with change size. Small PRs take seconds. Large changes with many files may take several minutes for thorough analysis.
